chapter_context.summary: Details three visions of judgment: locusts and fire (both averted by Amos's intercession) and a plumbline (unbending judgment), followed by a historical confrontation where the priest Amaziah accuses Amos of conspiracy and commands him to flee Judah.

outline.text: The First Vision: Locusts Devouring the Latter Growth, Averted by Amos's Intercession
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The First Vision: Locusts Devouring the Latter Growth, Averted by Amos's Intercession (7:1-3), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Amos.

outline.text: The Second Vision: The Devouring Fire Consuming the Deep, Averted by Amos's Intercession
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Second Vision: The Devouring Fire Consuming the Deep, Averted by Amos's Intercession (7:4-6), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Amos.

outline.text: The Third Vision: The Plumbline of Uncompromising and Unbending Judgment
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Third Vision: The Plumbline of Uncompromising and Unbending Judgment (7:7-9), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Amos.

outline.text: The Confrontation: Amaziah the Priest's Accusations and Amos's Defense and Doom Prophecy
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Confrontation: Amaziah the Priest's Accusations and Amos's Defense and Doom Prophecy (7:10-17), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Amos.

key_themes.title: Social Justice and Equity
key_themes.description: Amos fiercely denounces religious ritual that exists alongside economic exploitation, emphasizing that true worship requires justice for the poor.

key_themes.title: The Danger of Spiritual Complacency
key_themes.description: The book warns that material prosperity can easily blind a nation to its spiritual and ethical decline, leading to swift judgment.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the indictments and visions of Chapter 7, how can you actively champion justice and care for the needy in your community today?
How can we guard ourselves against the kind of complacency and empty religious formalism that Amos rebuked in Samaria?

cultural_historical.title: The Reign of Jeroboam II
cultural_historical.text: Amos prophesied during the mid-8th century BC, a period of remarkable material prosperity, territory expansion, and luxurious stability for the Northern Kingdom under Jeroboam II, which unfortunately saw extreme wealth disparity and systemic social oppression.
